blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I know him. I know who he is.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: How do you know someone like that?

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Because I owe him money. I've owed him money for eight months.
The room went the particular quiet of people who already knew something was coming and still weren't ready when it arrived.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: You told us thirty-one thousand. You told us it was gambling.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: It is gambling. Price is who you end up with when the original lender sells your debt. I didn't go to him directly. It doesn't work that way.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Yes. Marcus knew. He was the only one I told. He was trying to help me figure out how to handle it.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I don't know exactly. He had a plan. He didn't share all the details with me because he said the less I knew the better while he was working on it.
Davenport had not stopped watching Cade since the conversation started.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Mr. Mercer, I'd like to speak with you separately. Now, if you don't mind.
